input.command:
kubectl get svc -n elasticsearch
echo "=== ingress-nginx ==="
kubectl get pods,svc -n ingress-nginx
echo "=== existing ingress ==="
kubectl get ingress -A
echo "=== ingressclass ==="
kubectl get ingressclass

--enable-metrics=false
      --publish-status-address=localhost
      --watch-ingress-without-class=false
    Requests:
      cpu:      100m
      memory:   90Mi
    Liveness:   http-get http://:10254/healthz delay=10s timeout=1s period=10s #success=1 #failure=5
    Readiness:  http-get http://:10254/healthz delay=10s timeout=1s period=10s #success=1 #failure=3
    Environment:
      POD_NAME:       ingress-nginx-controller-b7b87d69c-bllwr (v1:metadata.name)
      POD_NAMESPACE:  ingress-nginx (v1:metadata.namespace)
      LD_PRELOAD:     /usr/local/lib/libmimalloc.so
    Mounts:
      /usr/local/certificates/ from webhook-cert (ro)
      /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount from kube-api-access-fzlzp (ro)
Conditions:
  Type           Status
  PodScheduled   False 
Volumes:
  webhook-cert:
    Type:        Secret (a volume populated by a Secret)
    SecretName:  ingress-nginx-admission
    Optional:    false
  kube-api-access-fzlzp:
    Type:                    Projected (a volume that contains injected data from multiple sources)
    TokenExpirationSeconds:  3607
    ConfigMapName:           kube-root-ca.crt
    Optional:                false
    DownwardAPI:             true
QoS Class:                   Burstable
Node-Selectors:              ingress-ready=true
                             kubernetes.io/os=linux

                             node-role.kubernetes.io/master:NoSchedule

input.command:
kubectl get nodes --show-labels | tr ',' '\n' | grep -i ingress
kubectl get nodes -o name

kubectl label node karma-7-control-plane ingress-ready=true
kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx -w &
WPID=$!
sleep 30
kill $WPID 2>/dev/null
kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx -o wide

description:

kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx -w &
WPID=$!
sleep 30
kill $WPID 2>/dev/null
kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx -o wide

content.text: Controller scheduled and is running. Now I need a TLS secret for `es.example.com` and an Ingress with TLS configured (so HTTPS works and plain HTTP is blocked via `ssl-redirect`/force-ssl-redirect, which is the default nginx behavior when TLS is set).

input.command:
kubectl get pod -n ingress-nginx -l app.kubernetes.io/component=controller -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.conditions}'; echo
kubectl logs -n ingress-nginx -l app.kubernetes.io/component=controller --tail=20
